activities_projects.title: Comparative Study of Rights in Different Countries
activities_projects.objective: Analyze differences in democratic rights across countries
steps: Select two democratic countries and one non-democratic country
Compare the rights provided to citizens
Present findings in a comparison table
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze global differences in rights

activities_projects.title: Mock Court Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand judicial process in protecting rights
steps: Create a mock case involving violation of rights
Assign roles like judge, lawyer, and victim
Conduct a mock trial and give a verdict

activities_projects.title: Rights and Responsibilities Essay
activities_projects.objective: Understand the relationship between rights and duties
steps: Write an essay on why rights must be balanced with responsibilities
Include examples from daily life
Explain consequences of misuse of rights
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking about rights

activities_projects.title: Fundamental Rights Case Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze court cases related to Fundamental Rights
steps: Research one Supreme Court case related to Fundamental Rights
Explain the issue and the judgment
Discuss its impact on society
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand legal interpretations of rights

activities_projects.title: Human Rights Campaign
steps: Design pamphlets explaining human rights
Organize a small awareness campaign in school
Explain rights to other students

activities_projects.title: Rights Expansion Research
activities_projects.objective: Understand how rights expand over time
steps: Research new rights such as Right to Information and Right to Education
Explain how they evolved from Fundamental Rights
Prepare a report

activities_projects.title: Human Rights Violation Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze real-life cases of rights violations
steps: Select a real case of human rights violation
Explain which rights were violated
Suggest legal remedies
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students evaluate legal and ethical issues

activities_projects.title: Rights Web Diagram
steps: Draw circles representing each Fundamental Right
Connect related rights using arrows
Provide examples explaining the connections

activities_projects.title: Public Interest Litigation Study
steps: Research one famous Public Interest Litigation case
Explain why PIL was filed
Discuss its outcome
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand role of citizens in protecting rights

activities_projects.title: Policy Suggestion Report
activities_projects.objective: Develop solutions to protect rights in society
steps: Identify a common rights violation issue
Propose policies to solve the issue
Present suggestions in a written report
